items.title: Micron vs. Intel: Which AI Stock Is the Better Buy?
items.summary: Both Micron and Intel have surged over 200% year-to-date on AI tailwinds. Intel is positioned for future growth through its AI chips, CPUs, and foundry services, with government support. However, Micron emerges as the better buy due to its explosive current growth—quadrupling revenue year-over-year with a 7.4 forward P/E ratio, compared to Intel's 147 P/E and 7% revenue growth.

items.title: Broadcom vs. Navitas Semiconductor: Which AI Chip Maker Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
items.summary: Broadcom and Navitas Semiconductor represent two contrasting approaches to AI chip investing. Broadcom dominates with $63.9B in revenue, strong profitability, and 66% projected growth in 2026, while Navitas pursues a risky pivot toward GaN power chips for data centers and EVs, currently unprofitable with declining revenue. The article recommends Broadcom as the safer choice with better valuation metrics and proven execution.

items.title: Applied Materials Is Silently Powering the AI Boom. Here's Why.
items.summary: Applied Materials, the largest U.S. semiconductor equipment provider, is positioned to benefit from the AI boom through partnerships with major chipmakers like TSMC, Micron, and SK Hynix. The company expects 30% revenue growth in its semiconductor segment for calendar 2026, up from 10.4% year-over-year growth in Q2, driven by a multiyear AI supercycle. However, the stock's P/E ratio has surged to over 50 from the mid-teens a year ago, leaving limited margin of safety despite strong fundamentals.

items.title: S&P 500 Earnings Depend on AI Infrastructure Doing the Heavy Lifting
items.summary: The S&P 500 is on track for its best quarter since Q2 2020 with over 14% returns. Q2 earnings are expected to grow around 23-30%, heavily driven by AI infrastructure stocks and memory chip makers. Technology sector, particularly NVIDIA and Micron, is expected to contribute 60% of S&P 500 EPS growth. Profit margin expansion across sectors will be critical to sustaining this earnings growth momentum.

items.title: Has Stock Market Exuberance Become Irrational?
items.summary: The stock market has rallied strongly in Q2 2026 with the S&P 500 up 12.6% and Nasdaq-100 up over 20%. While sentiment indicators like the AAII survey remain near historical averages and positioning has moderated from extreme levels, record margin debt ($1.42 trillion) and overbought conditions in semiconductors suggest elevated optimism. The analysis concludes the market shows signs of a mature bull market due for a pause rather than irrational exuberance, with semiconductor stocks particularly stretched at 64% premium to their 40-week moving average.

items.title: 2 Hidden Industrial Stocks That Will Benefit From the AI Infrastructure Build-Out Not Named SpaceX or Intel
items.summary: The article highlights ON Semiconductor and Rockwell Automation as undervalued industrial stocks poised to benefit from AI inference spending growth. ON Semiconductor's AI data center revenue is expected to double from $250M (2025) to $500M (2026), while Rockwell Automation is embedding AI inference models into its industrial automation solutions through partnerships with Nvidia. Both companies currently have low AI exposure but are positioned for significant long-term growth as the AI market shifts from infrastructure investment to inference deployment.

items.title: How Buying Penguin Solutions Today Could 10X Your Net Worth
items.summary: Penguin Solutions, a memory and AI infrastructure company, shows promise despite a 6% year-over-year revenue decline. The decline is attributed to legacy business slowdown, while its high-growth integrated memory segment surged 63% YoY, driven by AI and high-performance computing customer deals. The company recently received Nvidia's AI factory partner designation, positioning it for potential significant growth.

items.title: AI’s Hidden Cost Problem Is Making Memory Chip Makers the Biggest Winners
items.summary: As AI infrastructure buildout accelerates, memory chip suppliers (Micron, Samsung, SK Hynix) have become critical bottlenecks, commanding pricing power similar to oil producers during an airline boom. While hyperscalers absorb rising memory costs to maintain competitive advantage, the economics remain unsustainable long-term. The AI profit pool is fragmenting, with memory suppliers capturing disproportionate gains until efficiency improvements, better monetization, or new supply capacity arrives.

items.title: If AI Is Such a Sure Thing, Why Are So Many Insiders Selling?
items.summary: Despite soaring public enthusiasm for AI investments, prominent insiders including Stanley Druckenmiller, Peter Thiel, and Michael Burry are quietly selling or hedging their AI-related positions. Industry experts warn the AI bubble could be more explosive than the dot-com peak, raising questions about whether insider actions reflect rational caution about future expectations.

items.title: Is Micron Stock the New Nvidia?
items.summary: Micron Technology has emerged as a major AI beneficiary, providing essential memory and storage products for AI systems. With revenue quadrupling to over $41 billion and net income reaching $28 billion in the latest quarter, Micron's stock has surged over 800% in the past year. While the company faces competition from SK Hynix in AI memory, strong demand exceeding supply and high gross margins (84%) position Micron similarly to Nvidia's growth trajectory, though with potentially more risk.

items.title: As SpaceX Falls Down to Earth, the Stock Is Still Not a Buy
items.summary: SpaceX's stock has pulled back to opening-day levels after an initial strong start following its IPO as the largest company ever to go public. The article warns investors to avoid the stock due to upcoming lock-up expirations that could pressure shares, a high valuation relative to revenue, and heavy reliance on speculative future ventures like space-based AI data centers.

items.title: The AI Memory Supercycle Could Last Longer Than Investors Expect. That's Great News for Micron and Sandisk.
items.summary: Micron's Q3 FY2026 earnings significantly exceeded expectations with $41.5B in revenue versus $33.5B guidance, suggesting the AI-driven memory chip supercycle is still in early stages and likely to extend longer than anticipated. The company's record-level investments and multiyear customer agreements indicate sustained demand, while Sandisk is expected to report similarly strong results. These developments suggest the memory supercycle will maintain elevated prices and high margins for extended periods.

items.title: 3 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks I'd Buy Now and Never Sell
items.summary: The article recommends three AI stocks for long-term investment: Arm Holdings for its energy-efficient chip architecture dominating data centers, IREN for its data center capacity expansion with major contracts from Microsoft and Nvidia, and Nvidia for its leadership in AI hardware and diversified revenue streams. With Morgan Stanley projecting $3 trillion in global data center construction costs through 2028, these companies are positioned to benefit from sustained AI infrastructure growth.

items.title: 3 Beaten-Down AI Chip Stocks to Consider Buying in the Sell-Off
items.summary: AI chip stocks have experienced a significant sell-off due to concerns about whether AI spending will justify industry investments. The article examines three beaten-down chip stocks—Nvidia, ON Semiconductor, and Intel—as potential buying opportunities. Nvidia trades at 29x earnings with strong 85% revenue growth; ON Semiconductor fell 23% after announcing a $7 billion acquisition but shows signs of cyclical recovery; Intel has surged on turnaround efforts but remains unprofitable on a GAAP basis. The author favors Nvidia as the least risky of the three.

items.title: Is the Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) a Buy Before July 10?
items.summary: SK Hynix plans to list American depositary receipts on Nasdaq on July 10, targeting $29.4 billion in proceeds for chip fabrication expansion. The Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) provides indirect exposure to SK Hynix and other AI memory leaders. While the listing is a meaningful catalyst, the article advises against timing purchases around this event, recommending dollar-cost averaging instead as the market has already priced in the expected development.

items.title: Here's Why Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) May Be the Smartest AI Infrastructure Buy Right Now
items.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) is positioned as the premier AI infrastructure investment, controlling over 70% of the global foundry market. The company benefits from strong AI chip demand, high margins (47% net profit margin), and significant switching costs for customers. With stock price up 110% over the past year and Q1 revenue surging 40% year-over-year, TSMC is raising prices amid strong demand, reinforcing its competitive moat despite growing competition from Intel and Samsung.

items.title: How Intel Is Packaging the Future of American Chips
items.summary: Intel has surged 281.8% year-to-date by pivoting to become a sovereign foundry powerhouse, with a new advanced packaging division led by SK hynix veteran Seok-Hee Lee. The company secured preliminary agreements with Apple for M-series chip production and a $5 billion strategic investment from NVIDIA. However, Intel trades at a stretched 223x forward P/E ratio with massive operating deficits, requiring flawless execution and near-term margin compression to justify current valuations.

items.title: Micron Profits and Apple Prices Reveal AI’s Hidden Inflation Problem
items.summary: While AI is expected to boost productivity and lower inflation long-term, the massive investment boom in AI infrastructure is creating near-term inflationary pressures. Micron's strong earnings reflect surging demand for advanced chips, but this has caused memory costs to spike, forcing Apple to raise prices on Mac and iPad models. The article warns that major structural shifts like AI create bottlenecks and pricing pressures before supply catches up, complicating the Federal Reserve's inflation outlook.

items.title: Micron Prints Perfect, and Now the Chart Has to Answer
items.summary: Micron delivered exceptional Q3 results with $41.5B revenue (up 346% YoY), 84.9% gross margin, and EPS of $25.11 beating expectations. The company guided Q4 to $50B revenue and secured $100B in long-term take-or-pay contracts through 2030, providing structural support. However, the stock's reaction remains uncertain as it trades within a rising channel, with the key question being whether the market views this as peak growth or a sign of continued acceleration.

items.title: SpaceX (SPCX) Stock Is Plunging. Should You Buy the Dip?
items.summary: SpaceX shares have fallen nearly 20% from their June 16 peak after surging 67% at IPO. Despite the dip, the author recommends against buying, citing an extremely high price-to-sales ratio of 77 (compared to Nvidia's 20 and Micron's 24), lack of profitability, and concerns about planned debt and equity dilution. The stock is priced for perfection with no margin of safety.

items.title: Nvidia Should Be on Your Buy List if You Are This Type of Investor
items.summary: Nvidia, with its $4.9 trillion market cap, is now positioned to attract younger, risk-averse investors seeking growth without excessive risk. The company demonstrates strong fundamentals including 85% revenue growth, a modest P/E ratio of 32, and $80 billion in liquidity. While its low dividend yield (0.14%) makes it less attractive to income investors, Nvidia's recent 2,400% dividend increase signals growing commitment to shareholders, making it suitable for conservative growth investors.

items.title: System on Module Market Size to Hit USD 6.81 Billion by 2035 | Research by SNS Insider
items.summary: The global System on Module (SOM) market, valued at USD 3.00 billion in 2025, is expected to grow to USD 6.81 billion by 2035 at a CAGR of 8.57%. Growth is driven by expanding IoT adoption, edge AI technologies, and industrial automation. ARM Architecture dominates with 72.35% market share, while COM-HPC is the fastest-growing standard. North America leads regionally, with Asia-Pacific showing the fastest growth.

items.title: Micron’s Blowout Is About to Test the Summer Fade the Rally Crowd
items.summary: Micron delivered a blowout earnings report with revenue of $41.46B (vs. $36B expected) and guided Q4 revenue to ~$50B (vs. $43B Street estimate). The company signed 16 multi-year Strategic Customer Agreements worth ~$100B in contracted revenue, signaling a structural shift in the memory industry from cyclical boom-bust patterns to more stable, contracted cash flows. This demonstrates that AI demand continues to outrun memory supply, giving producers strong pricing power. However, investors should note that betting against Micron now requires believing that shortages, hyperscaler spending, and customer urgency all cool simultaneously.

items.title: Could SpaceX Stock Make You a Millionaire? This Is the Simple Answer
items.summary: SpaceX's $2 trillion valuation following its record $75 billion IPO limits its potential for explosive returns. While the company has ambitious plans for interplanetary travel and a $28.5 trillion addressable market, achieving 10x returns would require it to become the world's most valuable company by a significant margin—a difficult feat given the law of large numbers. Most wealth creation from SpaceX occurred in private markets before the IPO.

items.summary: The article compares SpaceX and Nvidia as AI investment options. While SpaceX recently acquired xAI and owns the X social media platform, its AI division generated only $3.2 billion in revenue with 22% growth. Nvidia, by contrast, is growing at 85% year-over-year with 96% projected growth in the current quarter, generating over $250 billion in annual revenue. Despite SpaceX's $2 trillion market cap being 40% of Nvidia's $5 trillion valuation, SpaceX's 2025 revenue was less than $20 billion with $6.6 billion in adjusted EBITDA—far below expectations for its valuation. The author concludes Nvidia is the better AI stock choice due to more reasonable valuation and stronger AI business fundamentals.

items.title: SpaceX Valuation Loses Altitude as AI Risks Mount
items.summary: SpaceX's stock has fallen 25% from its $225 IPO peak to $165, as investors reassess the company's strategic pivot toward AI data centers and software acquisitions. The $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Anysphere, combined with a $6.3 billion computing power agreement with Reflection AI, is straining cash flow and diluting shareholder value. Additionally, Starlink's average revenue per user has declined from $99 to $66 monthly, while the company faces potential merger rumors with Tesla. Rising interest rates and a microscopic 5% public float are amplifying the sell-off.

items.title: This Nvidia-Backed Company Just Entered the $154 Billion AI Photonics Market. Does This Make the $13 Stock a No-Brainer Buy?

Goldman Sachs projects the optical networking market tied to AI will grow from $15 billion in 2026 to $154 billion by 2028, with co-packaged optics representing a $91 billion opportunity. Recent insider buying by Nokia executives, combined with Nvidia's $1 billion strategic investment, suggests the stock has further upside potential.

items.title: Nvidia Just Raised $25 Billion in Debt. Here's What That Really Tells Investors

The move allows the company to lock in cheap long-term borrowing rates (4.25%-5.6%) while maintaining financial flexibility for shareholder returns and strategic investments. The strong investor demand for bonds maturing to 2056 signals confidence in sustained AI demand.

items.title: Rumble Closes Acquisition of Northern Data
items.summary: Rumble (NASDAQ: RUM) has completed its acquisition of Northern Data AG, gaining approximately 85.2% ownership. The deal provides Rumble with over 200 MW of unmonetized energy capacity, roughly 22,000 NVIDIA GPUs, and access to one of Europe's largest independent GPU estates. Northern Data raised its 2026 revenue outlook to 170-190 million euros from 130-150 million euros, with GPU utilization reaching 85%. Rumble also announced a $270 million multi-year contract with Together AI for GPU cloud capacity.

While the Fed is expected to leave rates unchanged at this meeting, Warsh's policy direction—including potential balance sheet reduction and less forward guidance—could significantly impact the stock market, particularly given historically expensive valuations.

This trend may favor diversified and equal-weight semiconductor ETFs over mega-cap concentrated funds.

items.title: From Crypto to AI: Insiders Are Trading These 3 Stocks
items.summary: Insider trading activity reveals mixed signals across three major stocks. Robinhood Markets shows bullish insider buying after a 25% decline in 2026, with $35M in Q2 purchases marking the first significant buys since Q2 2025. Snowflake experienced insider selling spikes following its impressive earnings beat and 36% single-day surge, though most sales were under predetermined plans. Navitas Semiconductor, up over 200% in 2026 due to its NVIDIA partnership, saw $116M in insider sales—its highest quarterly sales in three years—signaling caution despite strong performance.

items.title: Massive News: Iren's $4.4 Billion AI Target Could Change Everything
items.summary: Iren is positioning itself as a major AI infrastructure player with Blackwell systems and data center capacity, targeting multibillion-dollar revenue. While execution could deliver powerful upside, the stock's current valuation already reflects high expectations for success, leaving limited margin for error.

items.title: Fortinet's 74% Surge Since April Is Attracting Attention, but Is It Sustainable?
items.summary: Fortinet has surged 74% since April, driven by accelerating revenue growth (20% YoY in Q1) and strong tailwinds from AI infrastructure expansion. The cybersecurity company is benefiting from growing attack surfaces created by AI adoption, partnerships with AI leaders, and its unique custom security chips. With over $700 billion expected in big tech AI spending and 800+ AI data centers under construction, Fortinet appears positioned for sustained growth outpacing the broader cybersecurity industry.

items.title: AI Juggernauts Nvidia, Palantir, Micron, and Broadcom Are Sending Shockwaves Through Wall Street With This Nearly $13 Billion Warning
items.summary: Despite massive stock rallies since 2023, insiders at major AI companies Nvidia, Palantir, Micron, and Broadcom have collectively sold nearly $13 billion in shares over three years while making minimal purchases. This insider selling activity, combined with historically unsustainable valuations (Palantir trading at 67x sales), suggests potential concerns about future growth and possible bubble conditions in AI stocks.

items.title: OpenAI To Surpass $1.5 Trillion On Debut, Join Elite 'Mag 7' Ranks? Crypto Punters Lift Odds After Confidential IPO Filing
items.summary: OpenAI has filed a confidential IPO registration statement with the SEC. Cryptocurrency prediction markets now assign a 48% probability that OpenAI will debut with a valuation exceeding $1.5 trillion, up from 42% the previous day. The company is currently valued at $852 billion following a $122 billion funding round in March.

items.title: Diraq Appoints Scott A. McGregor as New Chairman Amid U.S. Expansion
items.summary: Quantum computing company Diraq announced the appointment of Scott A. McGregor, former CEO of Broadcom, as Chairman of the Board. The company also secured a $38 million Letter of Intent from the U.S. Department of Commerce under the CHIPS Act to scale its silicon-based quantum processors. Diraq aims to deliver its first quantum computing product by 2029.

items.title: Is Marvell Stock a Buy Before June 22?
items.summary: Marvell Technology was selected for S&P 500 inclusion, effective June 22, 2026. The stock has surged 210% year-to-date following Nvidia CEO's endorsement as a potential trillion-dollar AI chip company. The article advises using dollar-cost averaging rather than trying to time the index rebalance event, emphasizing the long-term AI growth opportunity over short-term momentum trading.

items.title: Better Buy After the Semiconductor Sell-Off: Marvell or Broadcom?
items.summary: After recent semiconductor sell-offs, Broadcom emerges as the better buy compared to Marvell. While both companies design custom AI chips for cloud operators, Broadcom demonstrates faster revenue growth (48% YoY), higher AI revenue contribution (nearly 50% of sales), and a more reasonable valuation (64x earnings vs. Marvell's 90x earnings). Marvell's recent 32% surge followed Nvidia CEO's endorsement, but the stock appears overvalued with heavy reliance on a small customer base.

items.title: If You'd Invested $10,000 in Nvidia Stock 10 Years Ago, Here's How Much You'd Have Today
items.summary: Nvidia has delivered extraordinary returns over the past decade, turning a $10,000 investment into nearly $1.9 million with an 18,720% total return. The AI hardware leader commands a dominant market position in data center chips and software, boasting a 65.6% operating margin and a $5.3 trillion market cap. Despite its massive gains, the stock trades at a reasonable 33.5 P/E ratio, outperforming even Bitcoin's 11,040% gain over the same period.

items.summary: Despite strong earnings with 85% revenue growth and 306% net income increase, Palantir's stock dropped 7% due to its extremely high valuation multiples (P/E of 180, forward P/E of 110). The article recommends the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) as a less risky alternative for AI exposure, citing its 76% year-to-date return, diversified portfolio of 25 chipmakers, and lower P/E ratio of 49.

items.title: Prediction: This Artificial Intelligence Semiconductor Stock Will Outperform Nvidia Over the Next 5 Years
items.summary: CoreWeave, an AI cloud infrastructure provider and Nvidia partner, is positioned to outperform Nvidia over the next five years due to faster revenue growth (112% YoY) driven by a massive $99.4 billion backlog. Despite significant losses and high debt, CoreWeave's smaller revenue base and the projected 31% CAGR for AI through 2033 suggest strong growth potential, though the company faces profitability challenges and financial risk if AI demand falters.

items.title: Nvidia's 2,500% Dividend Hike Sounds Huge, but Here's What It Really Means for Investors
items.summary: Nvidia announced a 2,500% dividend increase from $0.01 to $0.25 per share, but the move is largely symbolic. With a yield of only around 0.4%, the hike reflects the company's focus on growth and capital allocation signaling rather than providing meaningful income to investors.

items.title: Why Planet Labs Stock Is Plummeting Lower This Week
items.summary: Planet Labs stock fell 35% this week following Q1 earnings and a $1.5 billion equity offering announcement. While Q1 results were solid, the market had priced the stock for perfection after an eightfold rise over the past year. The equity offering could dilute shareholders by 9%, though management is capitalizing on the elevated share price. Despite the decline, the company remains fundamentally sound with innovative AI-powered satellite imaging technology, but trades at a lofty 32x sales valuation.

items.title: NVIDIA CEO Says AI's Future Isn't Just Copper
items.summary: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang challenges the popular copper supercycle narrative, arguing that AI infrastructure will use a hybrid approach combining copper and optical technologies. While copper demand will grow, engineering innovations like 800-volt systems and fiber optics will reduce copper intensity per data center. The article highlights that technological evolution and infrastructure constraints limit unlimited copper demand growth.

items.title: Nvidia Turns Matchmaker as AI Companies Scramble for Data Center Space and Electricity
items.summary: Nvidia is expanding its role beyond chip supply by helping AI companies secure data center space and electricity infrastructure. CFO Colette Kress noted that compute capacity remains tight despite strong AI returns, with hyperscalers accounting for roughly half of Nvidia's revenue. AI cloud providers are emerging as a key growth driver, serving customers lacking scale to build their own infrastructure.

items.title: SpaceX’s IPO Will Test How Investors Price Vision
items.summary: SpaceX's upcoming IPO at a reported $1.75 trillion valuation presents a significant challenge for investors in pricing a company whose future potential far exceeds current financials. The article examines whether the valuation leaves adequate margin of safety given SpaceX's diverse business segments (launch services, Starlink, defense, AI infrastructure) and the difficulty of finding comparable companies for traditional valuation methods.

items.title: Is Nvidia Stock Too Cheap to Ignore?
items.summary: Despite Nvidia's 1,800% surge since 2022 and $5.4 trillion market cap, the article argues the AI chip giant remains attractively valued with a P/E ratio of 34 and forward multiple of 25, supported by 85% revenue growth. While another 1,800% gain is unlikely, the company is positioned to outperform the S&P 500's long-term average and deliver outsize returns from its massive data center growth.

items.title: After Dell, TSMC CEO Issues Stark Warning On AI Capacity Constraints For 'Very Long Time,' Hints At Potential Price Hike— 'We Still Need To...'
items.summary: TSMC CEO C.C. Wei warned of significant AI capacity constraints lasting a 'very long time' and signaled openness to raising prices for advanced chips. The company is considering a 15% price increase for 3nm chips in H2 2026 and potentially another 10% in 2027. Despite rising costs, customers remain positive about AI's future, though fully satisfying U.S. domestic production demands will take considerable time.

items.title: Stock Market Today: Dow Futures Fall, Nasdaq Gains As Middle East Tensions Intensify— Palo Alto, Marvell In Focus (UPDATED)
items.summary: U.S. stock futures showed mixed performance on Wednesday following a record close on Tuesday, with the Dow Jones and S&P 500 declining slightly while the Nasdaq 100 remained flat. Middle East tensions escalated as the U.S. military retaliated against Iran with strikes near the Strait of Hormuz. Key movers included Palo Alto Networks falling despite beating earnings estimates, Marvell Technology surging 12.67% after Nvidia's CEO called it a 'next trillion-dollar company,' and Broadcom advancing ahead of earnings. Treasury yields held steady at 4.48% for the 10-year bond, with markets pricing in a 98.4% probability of unchanged Fed rates in June.

items.title: Nvidia Has Racked Up a Nearly $20 Billion Profit Over the Last 5 Months From an Unlikely Source
items.summary: Nvidia has generated nearly $20 billion in profits over five months from its investment in Intel, which has quintupled in value since Nvidia's $5 billion purchase at $23.28/share in December to Intel's May closing price of $114.68/share. Beyond its core high-margin data center GPU business, Nvidia's strategic investment in and partnership with Intel for AI infrastructure platforms has proven highly beneficial for both companies.

items.title: Is Bittensor (TAO) the Next Crypto to Go Mainstream?
items.summary: Bittensor (TAO), an AI-focused cryptocurrency with a $2.8 billion market cap, is gaining momentum with a 15% year-to-date increase. The article suggests two catalysts could drive mainstream adoption: surpassing Shiba Inu's market cap to enter the top 25 cryptocurrencies, and the launch of spot Bittensor ETFs. With limited coin supply (11 million circulating, 21 million maximum) and backing from tech leaders like Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, Bittensor is positioned as a play on decentralized AI, though crypto investments carry substantial risk.

items.title: S&P 500: Options Markets Reflect Growing Macro Risk
items.summary: The S&P 500 rose slightly by 13 basis points, with semiconductors leading gains. However, the market shows significant internal disparity, with the XLK ETF outperforming by 21.2% over 30 days. Options markets suggest index volatility is relatively cheap while correlation risk remains elevated, indicating potential for a sharp market correction if macro shocks occur. Bitcoin fell 6.5% on settlement day with further downside expected.

items.title: Marvell Gets The Headlines — Coherent Stock Gets The All-Time High
items.summary: Following NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang's bullish commentary on AI infrastructure and optical connectivity demand in data centers, Marvell Technology surged 25% while Coherent Corp. quietly hit an all-time high with a 16% gain. Despite similar exposure to the optical transceiver trade, Coherent remains overlooked by investors despite its direct revenue exposure to AI optical demand. Coherent has rallied 455% from its 52-week low of $76.88 to $426.67.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX Just Made An IPO Filing Change That Has Tesla Investors Buzzing
items.summary: SpaceX filed an amended S-1 with the SEC ahead of its IPO later this month. The filing reveals SpaceX could issue significant equity for future transactions, potentially signaling a Tesla merger. Notably, any Tesla M&A could render Musk's $1 trillion pay package milestones obsolete, with compensation depending solely on market capitalization. SpaceX also announced deals with Anthropic (325,000 Nvidia GPUs) and American Airlines (Starlink Wi-Fi service).

items.title: 3 Reasons Why Sandisk Stock Can Still Go Higher
items.summary: Despite soaring over 600% in five months, Sandisk stock remains reasonably valued at under 10x forward earnings. The company benefits from strong demand for NAND memory in AI data centers, with revenue up 251% year-over-year and projected adjusted EPS of $175.62 for fiscal 2027. With AI infrastructure spending expected to reach $3-4 trillion by 2030, Sandisk is positioned for sustained growth.

items.title: Micron Joins the Trillion-Dollar Club on Surging AI Memory Demand
items.summary: Micron Technology reached a trillion-dollar market cap in late May 2026, driven by surging demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) in AI data centers. The company reported fiscal Q2 2026 revenue of $23.86 billion (up 196% YoY) with 69% operating margins, and guided Q3 revenue of $33.5 billion with 81% gross margins. All 2026 HBM supply is sold out under multi-year contracts at robust pricing. However, investors should consider the company's unprecedented $25+ billion capex commitment and historical memory market volatility.

items.title: Microsoft’s 200-Day Test Could Decide If the Stock Has Bottomed
items.summary: Microsoft stock surged 5.5% to $450 on strong volume after Nvidia unveiled the N1X processor co-developed with Microsoft for a new RTX Spark line of Windows machines. The stock is testing its critical 200-day moving average ($449.60), which will determine if the downtrend reverses into a base formation. While fundamentals remain strong with Azure growing 40% and Copilot adoption reaching 20 million paid seats, margin compression from data center capex remains a concern.

items.title: AI Was Supposed To Kill Software Stocks: ServiceNOW Just Staged Its Best Rally On Record
items.summary: Enterprise software stocks have staged a dramatic reversal after months of AI-displacement fears. ServiceNow posted a historic 40% rally in four sessions, while the software sector ETF (IGV) climbed 15% in three days. The narrative shift was catalyzed by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's endorsement that the AI agent era represents one of the best moments to be a software company, as platforms will be essential infrastructure for AI agents to operate through.

items.title: Why CoreWeave Stock Is Up on Monday
items.summary: CoreWeave shares surged 14.3% after announcing successful validation of Nvidia's Vera Rubin NVL72 AI accelerator, which offers significant improvements over previous generation technology. The stock gained momentum following endorsements from Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang at Computex and Dell CEO Michael Dell's announcement of the first liquid-cooled Dell PowerEdge XE9812 server rack for CoreWeave.

items.title: S&P 500 Halts Gains As Oil Jumps 8% On Iran Ceasefire Standoff: Stock Market Today
items.summary: Crude oil surged nearly 8% to its best day since April following U.S.-Iran military escalation, causing Treasury yields to rise and stalling the S&P 500's record run. The benchmark held flat while energy stocks led gains and software/AI stocks rallied on Nvidia's RTX Spark announcement. FedEx plunged 18.5% due to a mechanical spin-off, while MGM Resorts jumped 15.2% on an IAC takeover bid.

items.title: Is Iren the Next Winner of Nvidia's Neocloud Spending Spree?
items.summary: Iren has rallied 45% year-to-date following a strategic partnership with Nvidia to deploy AI chips across up to 5 gigawatts of data centers. The deal includes a $3.4 billion five-year agreement for Nvidia to use 60 megawatts of Iren's capacity, plus Nvidia's option to purchase up to 30 million Iren shares at $70 per share. This partnership accelerates Iren's deployment timelines and revenue growth, similar to Nvidia's profitable investments in CoreWeave and Nebius.

items.title: Why This Fund Sold $35 Million of Bristow Group Amid a 40% Stock Surge
items.summary: South Dakota Investment Council sold 801,900 shares of Bristow Group (valued at $35.24 million) in Q1 2026, reducing its stake to 1.8% of reportable assets. Despite the sale, Bristow Group shares have surged 40% over the past year, outperforming the S&P 500's 28% gain. The company reported strong Q1 results with revenue of $388.7 million and maintained its 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance of $295-$325 million, citing growth opportunities in defense spending, energy security, and electric aircraft.

items.title: Jensen Huang Used 1 Word to Describe AI Demand. It Could Be the Most Important of 2026.
items.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang described AI demand as having 'gone parabolic,' driven by agentic AI systems entering real-world use. The company reported 85% revenue growth to $81.6B in fiscal Q1 2027, with data center revenue up 92%. Nvidia aggressively increased shareholder returns with a 25-fold dividend hike and $80B in new buyback authorization, while committing $145B in supply to meet anticipated demand through 2027. However, risks remain from potential customer pullbacks and competitors' custom silicon programs.

items.title: These Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks Have Crushed Nvidia in 2026 With Gains of 67% and 121%. They Can Still Soar Higher
items.summary: Lumentum Holdings and Applied Materials have significantly outperformed Nvidia in 2026, with gains of 121% and 67% respectively. Both companies benefit from massive AI infrastructure investments. Lumentum's optical components enable high-speed data center connectivity, with revenue up 72% year-over-year. Applied Materials supplies semiconductor manufacturing equipment with accelerating growth expected to continue, with earnings projected to jump 36% in the current quarter.

items.title: Is Micron Stock a Buy Before June 24?
items.summary: Micron Technology has rapidly reached a $1 trillion market cap in just 48 trading days, driven by strong AI-related memory demand and supply constraints. The company reported nearly tripled revenue to $23.8 billion and a 67.6% operating margin in its most recent quarter. Management expects supply tightness to persist beyond 2026, with customers only able to fill 60% of memory needs. The stock trades at a forward P/E of 16 and is positioned to move higher ahead of its June 24 earnings report.

items.title: Is Sandisk a Millionaire-Maker Stock?
items.summary: Sandisk's stock has surged 4,000% in 12 months driven by AI data center demand for memory chips, with fiscal Q3 revenue jumping 233% and gross margins reaching 78.4%. However, the memory industry has a history of boom-and-bust cycles, and investors worry elevated growth won't sustain if AI spending slows or supply catches up to demand.

items.title: What Is SMCI Stock Worth Without The Scandal Discount?
items.summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) stock has been trading at a significant discount since a March DOJ indictment involving board member Yih-Shyan Liaw, despite the company not being named as a defendant. The article argues the scandal discount is unjustified given strong Q2 financials (revenue up 123% YoY), active compliance improvements including a new chief compliance officer and forensic review, and collaboration with Taiwanese authorities that resulted in server seizures. SMCI trades at 21.7x earnings versus peers at 45x+, suggesting potential upside as the compliance turnaround story gains recognition.

items.title: Artificial Intelligence (AI) Is Moving Beyond Data Centers. Nvidia Has Already Turned This Opportunity Into a Multibillion-Dollar Business
items.summary: Nvidia is capitalizing on the shift of AI from data centers to edge devices through its physical AI business, which generated over $9 billion in trailing twelve-month revenue with a 50% quarterly growth rate. The company is partnering with major players like Uber for robotaxis and manufacturers for factory automation. Additionally, Nvidia's decision to sell Vera server CPUs as standalone products opens a potential $200 billion addressable market. With physical AI devices projected to reach 145 million cumulative shipments by 2035 and the market expected to generate $960 billion in annual revenue by 2033, Nvidia is well-positioned for sustained growth.

items.title: Companies Buying AI Servers Like It's 2020 Toilet Paper? Dell Exec Just Revealed How Wild The Rush Has Become
items.summary: Dell's COO Jeff Clarke revealed that companies are rushing to secure AI server capacity years in advance, prioritizing guaranteed supply over price negotiations. Customers are planning infrastructure needs 3-5 years ahead due to volatile component pricing and supply uncertainties. Dell posted Q1 revenue of $43.84B (beating $35.45B estimates) and adjusted EPS of $4.86 (vs. $2.94 expected), with strong Q2 guidance also exceeding expectations. Dell stock surged 38.15% in premarket trading.

items.title: Huge News for Nvidia Stock Investors
items.summary: Nvidia stock investors have reasons to celebrate recent developments. While some investors express concerns about the durability of demand for Nvidia's products, the company continues to strengthen its position as the GPU giant and is expanding into a new $200 billion market. Recent earnings and strategic announcements from CEO Jensen Huang have provided positive news for AI investors.

items.title: Intel Has Tripled in 2026. Here's 1 Reason It Could Keep Going.
items.summary: Intel's stock has surged 225% in 2026, driven by a shift in AI workloads toward CPU-intensive inference and agentic tasks. The company's data center and AI division revenue grew 22% year-over-year to $5.1 billion with operating margins doubling to 31%. However, at over 100x forward earnings, the valuation leaves little room for error, and the company still faces challenges including foundry losses and execution risks.

items.title: IREN Is Flipping the Switch from Bitcoin to AI
items.summary: IREN Limited is pivoting from cryptocurrency mining to AI infrastructure, securing a $1.6 billion hardware deal with Dell for NVIDIA Blackwell systems and a $3.4 billion five-year contract with NVIDIA. The company is leveraging its 750-megawatt Texas power facility to bypass data center interconnection queues, positioning itself as a specialized AI cloud provider. Management projects $4.4 billion in annualized revenue, driving a structural re-rating of the company's valuation multiple.

items.title: 3 Reasons to Buy Nvidia Stock in June
items.summary: Despite Nvidia's exceptional Q1 results (85% revenue growth to $81.6B, 139% earnings growth), the stock declined 5% post-earnings. The author argues this presents a buying opportunity, citing three reasons: (1) the market typically corrects undervaluation, (2) supply constraints and China trade restrictions limit current growth potential, and (3) the stock now trades at reasonable valuations (17x next year's earnings) while growing at 85%, supported by the company's $80B buyback authorization and 25x dividend increase.

items.title: Super Micro Jumps 10% As It Cracks Down On Smuggling With New Taiwan Alliance
items.summary: Super Micro Computer stock surged 10.37% to $42.15 as the company announced strengthened compliance controls and a partnership with Taiwanese authorities to prevent illegal diversion of its AI server technology. The move comes after Taiwan detained three people accused of smuggling Super Micro servers and follows NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang's call for improved export-control compliance. The stock benefited from both sector momentum in technology and investor relief over the company's proactive governance stance.

items.title: Nio Stock Rallies Wednesday, Shaking Off Beijing Regulatory Scrutiny
items.summary: NIO shares rallied 7.60% to $5.66 on Wednesday as risk appetite improved despite recent Beijing regulatory scrutiny on cross-border securities trading. The company posted strong Q1 results with 98.3% YoY delivery growth to 83,465 units and gross margin expansion to 19%, though sequential metrics showed weakness with a 33.1% drop in deliveries and 26.3% revenue decline. Technically, the stock remains in a tug-of-war zone between its moving averages with mixed momentum signals.

items.title: Billionaire Phillipe Laffont Recently Sold Oracle, Tesla, and Nvidia and Purchased a Stock Down 94% Since Its IPO in 2020
items.summary: Billionaire investor Philippe Laffont's hedge fund Coatue Management sold significant positions in Oracle, Tesla, and Nvidia during Q1 2026 amid concerns about AI valuations and capital expenditure guidance. The fund initiated a new speculative position in Lucid Group, the luxury EV maker down 94% since its 2020 IPO, despite the company's ongoing financial struggles and challenging EV market conditions.

items.title: ResVR Launches AI-Powered Assistant to Transform How Buyers Design and Purchase Homes
items.summary: ResVR announced the launch of ResVR Assistant™, an AI-powered conversational tool integrated into its 3D virtual home experiences. The assistant enables homebuyers to ask questions, explore customization options, and receive real-time guidance throughout their purchasing journey. The platform also provides builders with actionable insights into buyer behavior and decision-making patterns, helping improve conversion rates and sales strategies.

items.title: IREN Bets Big on Dell as It Races to Rewrite Its Own Story
items.summary: IREN, a former Bitcoin miner, is rapidly transforming into an AI cloud infrastructure company. The company signed a $1.6 billion procurement agreement with Dell for GPUs and equipment to support its growing AI cloud operations. IREN has secured major contracts including a $9.7 billion five-year deal with Microsoft and a $3.4 billion contract with Nvidia, positioning it with $3.1 billion in contracted annualized recurring revenue. However, recent quarterly results showed a 34% revenue miss and significant losses, with the company's success dependent on executing its ambitious data center buildout across multiple continents.

items.title: Cloud-Flation: Dell’s On-Premise AI Bet Pays Off
items.summary: A fundamental shift in AI infrastructure is underway as enterprises move from centralized cloud data centers to on-premise hardware due to unsustainable token consumption costs (320x increase). Dell Technologies is positioned to dominate this transition through its AI Factory partnership with NVIDIA, having secured over 5,000 enterprise customers with 1,000 acquired in Q1 FY2027. The company's $43 billion backlog and 95% year-to-date stock surge reflect strong momentum, though valuation concerns and narrower hardware margins present potential headwinds.

items.title: Before the Next Nvidia-Style Run, Here Are 3 AI Stocks Worth Watching
items.summary: As AI infrastructure spending expands beyond chip manufacturing toward data connectivity, three companies are positioned for significant growth: Astera Labs designs high-speed connectivity chips with 93% YoY revenue growth; Marvell Technology offers custom AI chips and optical networking with projected 30-40% revenue growth; and Credo Technology provides connectivity solutions with 201% YoY revenue growth. All three are capitalizing on the emerging bottleneck of efficient data movement in AI systems.

items.title: Is Nvidia the Most Underrated Dividend Growth Stock to Own?
items.summary: Nvidia announced a massive 2,400% dividend increase to $0.25 per share, bringing its yield to 0.47%. While the company's strong financials and low payout ratio provide room for future increases, the article suggests Nvidia remains primarily a growth stock rather than a dividend growth play, with most returns likely coming from share price appreciation rather than dividend income.

items.title: 80 Billion Reasons to Buy Nvidia After Its Monster Earnings Report
items.summary: Nvidia reported first-quarter earnings that exceeded Wall Street expectations and its own guidance. The company announced an additional $80 billion stock buyback authorization, signaling management confidence in sustained growth driven by AI infrastructure demand. With a forward P/E of 25x, the stock appears modestly valued relative to historical premiums, with emerging catalysts including inference workloads and agentic AI creating additional demand for Nvidia's GPUs and new CPU architectures.

items.title: Nvidia's Board Just Authorized an Additional $80 Billion Buyback. Here's What That Really Signals to Investors.
items.summary: Nvidia's board approved an $80 billion share buyback and increased its quarterly dividend 25-fold to $0.25, signaling management confidence despite risks. The company returned a record $20 billion to shareholders in Q1 while maintaining robust free cash flow of $48.6 billion. However, revenue loss in China and competition from customers building their own AI chips present significant headwinds.

items.title: Cerebras vs. Nvidia: Which AI Stock Is the Smarter Buy Right Now?
items.summary: Cerebras' impressive IPO has generated investor enthusiasm for its innovative wafer-scale AI chip technology. However, Nvidia remains the smarter investment choice due to its dominant 86% market share in AI data centers, $216 billion in annual revenue compared to Cerebras' $500 million, and proven profitability. While Cerebras shows promise with backing from OpenAI, it remains a risky bet compared to the established market leader.

items.title: Eight Weeks Up, And Even A Hawkish Fed Can't Stop It: This Week On Wall Street
items.summary: The S&P 500 achieved its eighth consecutive weekly gain despite a hawkish Federal Reserve signaling potential rate hikes if inflation remains sticky. Markets now price in an 82% probability of a rate hike by year-end. While manufacturing PMI hit a 48-month high driven by AI infrastructure spending, consumer sentiment plummeted to a historic low of 44.8, creating a disconnect between record stock markets and depressed Main Street conditions. Nvidia delivered record earnings with $81.62B revenue and $91B Q2 guidance, along with an $80B buyback.

items.title: Here's Why Navitas Semiconductor Soares Today (Hint: It's Nvidia Related)
items.summary: Navitas Semiconductor surged 17.27% following Nvidia's earnings report, which confirmed strength in the AI data center market. Navitas, an Nvidia partner developing power chips for next-generation 800V HVDC data centers, benefited as short sellers were forced to close positions after Nvidia's positive guidance. The stock's rise reflects trader confidence in Navitas's positioning for significant revenue growth in 2027.

items.title: Why the Smartest AI Money Is Quietly Moving Into Quantum Computing Right Now
items.summary: Institutional investors are beginning to shift capital toward quantum computing as the technology moves from theoretical research into practical commercial applications. Companies like Nvidia, IonQ, and Rigetti are demonstrating concrete technical milestones and early revenue traction, signaling that quantum computing may play a meaningful role in future AI infrastructure. However, the sector remains highly speculative with volatile valuations and uncertain timelines.

items.title: Lenovo's AI Revenue Surge Exposes Nvidia's China Blind Spot
items.summary: Lenovo reported record Q4 results with AI revenue growing 84% and now representing 38% of quarterly revenue. The company's $15.5 billion AI server pipeline and partnership with Nvidia position it as a full-stack AI infrastructure provider. Meanwhile, Nvidia's market share in China has collapsed to zero due to U.S. export controls, creating a structural advantage for Lenovo as a Hong Kong-headquartered company with Asian manufacturing ties.

items.title: SpaceX IPO Tests How Far Private Market Valuations Can Stretch
items.summary: SpaceX filed for its IPO targeting a $1.75-2 trillion valuation while only floating 5% of shares. The company generated $18.7B in revenue in 2025 but posted a $5B net loss, with profitable Starlink (9M subscribers, 50%+ margins) offsetting losses from Space and xAI divisions. Meanwhile, Nvidia beat earnings expectations but stock remained flat as gains were already priced in. Walmart reported good earnings but reduced guidance due to higher energy costs and tariff pressures, noting consumer caution particularly among lower-income earners.

items.title: Nvidia Just Raised Its Dividend By 2,400% -- and That Wasn't Even the Biggest News From Its Earnings Report
items.summary: Nvidia reported exceptional fiscal Q1 earnings with 85% year-over-year revenue growth to $81.6 billion, accelerating for three consecutive quarters. The company raised its quarterly dividend 25-fold from $0.01 to $0.25 per share and approved an additional $80 billion in share repurchases. Data center revenue hit a record $75.2 billion with networking revenue nearly tripling. Management guided for fiscal Q2 revenue of $91 billion, implying ~95% year-over-year growth.

items.title: NVIDIA Announces Upcoming Events for Financial Community
items.summary: NVIDIA reported record first quarter fiscal 2027 financial results with revenue of $81.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year, and Data Center revenue of $75.2 billion, up 92% year-over-year. The company also announced an $80.0 billion additional share repurchase authorization and appointed Suzanne Nora Johnson to its Board of Directors.

items.title: Is Nvidia's 25x Dividend Increase and New $80 Billion Share Repurchase Authorization a sign the Company Is Slowing Down?
items.summary: Nvidia reported strong Q1 FY2027 earnings with $1.87 adjusted EPS and $81.6B revenue, beating consensus estimates. The company raised its quarterly dividend 25-fold to $0.25/share and authorized an additional $80B in share repurchases. While these moves signal confidence and attract income investors, some worry they indicate fewer growth reinvestment opportunities. However, Nvidia continues showing impressive growth with 85% YoY revenue growth and 140% EPS growth, suggesting the company remains in expansion mode despite its massive $5.3T market cap.

items.title: Why Sandisk Stock Just Popped Again
items.summary: Sandisk stock jumped 6.2% as investors weighed mixed news. Nvidia's strong earnings growth (85% YoY sales surge) and high gross margins (74.9%) boosted demand outlook for NAND flash memory. However, Samsung's resolution of its 18-day labor strike and return to full production could increase competition and pricing pressure on Sandisk.

items.title: To Nvidia and Beyond: Sometimes Being Great Isn't Enough
items.summary: Nvidia delivered spectacular Q1 2027 results with 85% revenue growth and doubled adjusted earnings, but the market initially shrugged off the strong performance. Despite market apathy toward earnings announcements, Nvidia stock has gained 66% over the past year and is trading at a reasonable 19x forward earnings multiple. The author argues that long-term investors should focus on the company's sustained growth trajectory, strong demand, and AI revolution momentum rather than short-term post-earnings volatility.

items.title: Stock Market: Will S&P 500 Open Up Or Down Today?
items.summary: The S&P 500 snapped a three-day losing streak on Wednesday, rising 1.08% to 7,432.97, driven by cooling oil prices, falling Treasury yields, and optimism about Middle East conflict resolution. Nvidia beat earnings expectations and raised guidance, supporting the AI investment theme. Polymarket traders predict a 66% probability the index will open higher on Thursday. S&P 500 futures were up 0.09% early Thursday.

items.title: NVIDIA's Next Gold Mine? Jensen Huang Sees 'Brand New' $200 Billion Opportunity In Vera CPUs
items.summary: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang announced a new $200 billion market opportunity with the company's Vera CPU, designed for agentic AI workloads. The Vera CPU, which can be deployed standalone or alongside Rubin GPUs, has already generated $20 billion in sales this year. Huang expects billions of AI agents to require CPU-driven infrastructure, positioning NVIDIA to capitalize on growing CPU demand as AI shifts from training to inference.

items.title: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Gains Momentum on H200 China Clearance - Yahoo Finance
items.summary: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Gains Momentum on H200 China Clearance    Yahoo Finance

items.title: Nvidia Earnings Impact Tracker: Historical Data Shows Hidden Bull Trap
items.summary: Nvidia reports Q1 2027 earnings with Wall Street expecting $78.8B revenue and $1.77 EPS, representing 78% revenue growth. However, historical data reveals a concerning pattern: despite beating estimates in 17 consecutive quarters, the stock has shown weak immediate reactions, with negative median returns in the week following earnings. Recent reports show average 1-day reactions of -1.5% and 1-week reactions of -3.7%, suggesting the market no longer rewards beats alone and instead demands accelerating AI demand narratives.

items.title: Nvidia Earnings Preview: The AI Bellwether Faces Its Moment of Truth
items.summary: Nvidia is set to report fiscal Q1 earnings on Wednesday after market close, with consensus expectations for $78.8B revenue (80% YoY growth) and $1.75 EPS (116% YoY growth). The results will be closely watched as they could set the tone for the entire tech sector. Key focus areas include forward guidance, Blackwell chip ramp, gross margins, and China revenue. Options markets are pricing in a potential 7% stock swing. A beat with strong guidance could reignite the AI rally, while any softness could trigger a rotation out of tech.

items.title: Earnings Become Key as Markets Look Past High Oil and Interest Rates
items.summary: Markets are rebounding despite high oil prices and interest rates, with focus shifting to earnings and AI spending forecasts. NVIDIA's earnings report and CEO Jensen Huang's outlook are critical for market direction. Oil prices fell 4% following reports of ships successfully transiting the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran tensions. Growth and momentum stocks are leading, with the trend remaining positive contingent on NVIDIA's guidance and resolution of geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Stock Market Today, May 20: Nvidia Climbs Ahead of Earnings as Investors Await AI Guidance
items.summary: Major stock indices rose on May 20, 2026, as easing Treasury yields and softer oil prices fueled a market rebound. Nvidia climbed ahead of its after-hours earnings report, with expectations for strong guidance on Q2 revenue and gross margins. The broader market sentiment was supported by AI sector strength, with semiconductor and software stocks advancing.

items.title: Alibaba Takes Aim At Nvidia With New 3X Powerful AI Chip And Next-Gen LLM Model As China Tech War Heats Up
items.summary: Alibaba unveiled its Zhenwu M890 AI chip, delivering triple the performance of its predecessor with 144 GB GPU memory and 800 GB/s interchip bandwidth. The company has shipped 560,000 Zhenwu units to over 400 customers and announced its next-generation Qwen3.7-Max AI model. Alibaba's AI infrastructure investments aim to generate $4.42 billion in recurring revenue by year-end, challenging Nvidia's dominance in China's domestic AI processor market amid U.S. export restrictions.

items.title: The AI Stock That Could Turn a $10,000 Investment Into a Retirement-Changing Sum by 2030
items.summary: Silicon Motion Technology, a smaller AI stock with an $8 billion market cap, has nearly tripled year-to-date and crushed earnings expectations with 105% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026. The company produces NAND flash controllers critical to AI infrastructure and projects 20% sequential growth in Q2, positioning it as a potential high-growth investment opportunity by 2030.

items.title: Eli Lilly Stock: Next Stop $2,000?
items.summary: Eli Lilly's stock has surged above $1,000 following strong Q1 results driven by its dominance in weight-loss and diabetes markets. Despite increasing competition from rivals like Novo Nordisk and Amgen, the company maintains advantages through superior pipeline candidates like Retatrutide and diversified revenue streams in oncology, immunology, and neuroscience. AI-powered drug development efforts could further accelerate growth, potentially enabling the stock to double within six years.

items.title: Nvidia Doubled Down on Its Second-Largest AI Holding. Should Investors Follow Suit?
items.summary: Nvidia nearly doubled its stake in CoreWeave to 47.2 million shares (11% ownership) worth $4.9 billion in Q1 2026, signaling strong confidence in the neocloud provider. CoreWeave reported 112% year-over-year revenue growth to $2 billion and a $99.4 billion backlog, though the company remains unprofitable. The article suggests CoreWeave could be worth a small position in a balanced portfolio given Nvidia's mentorship and accelerating growth, though it trades at 8x sales compared to Nvidia's 27x forward earnings.

items.title: What Tim Cook's Trip to China Could Mean for Apple Investors
items.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook participated in a Trump administration delegation to Beijing, a significant trip for the company given its heavy reliance on Chinese manufacturing and the Chinese market. Easing tariffs and improved U.S.-China relations could benefit Apple by securing manufacturing relationships, reducing costs, and maintaining market access. Chinese President Xi Jinping signaled China will 'open wider' for business, and with Apple's stock at all-time highs above $300, investors should be cautiously optimistic about the trip's outcomes.

items.title: Better Buy: Micron or Nvidia Stock
items.summary: Nvidia and Micron are both thriving on AI-driven data center demand, but they present different investment profiles. Nvidia dominates GPU markets with sustainable competitive advantages and 79-86% projected revenue growth, making it a stable long-term AI play. Micron benefits from memory chip shortages with explosive 261-246% growth projections, but faces cyclical risks as supply catches up, potentially returning to single-digit P/E ratios. The choice depends on investor risk tolerance: Nvidia for set-and-forget stability, Micron for higher upside with volatility.

items.title: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, S&P 500 Futures Drop Following Drone Strike On UAE Nuclear Plant—Regeneron Pharma, NextNRG, ServiceNow In Focus
items.summary: U.S. stock futures fell on Monday following Friday's sharp declines, triggered by a drone strike near the UAE's Barakah nuclear power plant. While UAE officials confirmed no radiation leak or injuries, geopolitical tensions remain elevated. Key movers include Regeneron Pharmaceuticals tumbling 11.92% after failed Phase 3 trials, NextNRG surging 91.16% on strong quarterly revenue growth, and ServiceNow rising 3.50% following a partnership announcement with Experian. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 declined in premarket trading.

items.title: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Fell on Concerns Over the Sustainability of AI Infrastructure Capital Expenditure - Yahoo Finance
items.summary: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Fell on Concerns Over the Sustainability of AI Infrastructure Capital Expenditure    Yahoo Finance

items.title: 3 Quantum Computing Stocks to Buy Right Now
items.summary: Quantum computing is positioned as the next major technology trend after AI. The article recommends three stocks: D-Wave Quantum for its specialized quantum annealing approach and early commercial success, IonQ for holding the world record in quantum computing accuracy and strong revenue growth, and Nvidia for its hybrid computing strategy and supporting software platforms that will bridge quantum and traditional computing.

items.title: Is Micron the Next Nvidia?
items.summary: Micron Technology is experiencing strong AI-driven growth with Q2 fiscal 2026 revenue up 196% YoY and gross margins exceeding 70%, trading at a forward P/E of 7.4 versus the semiconductor industry median of 30x. However, while Micron is a critical supplier of high-bandwidth memory for AI accelerators, it lacks Nvidia's platform dominance and pricing power. Unlike Nvidia, which controls the AI ecosystem architecture, Micron is a commodity supplier vulnerable to price competition and cyclical margin compression as supply capacity increases.

items.title: Nvidia Stock Is Poised to Rocket From the Booming Space Economy
items.summary: Nvidia is positioned to dominate AI applications in the rapidly growing space economy, which is projected to reach $1.8 trillion by 2035. The company has launched space-hardened AI computing platforms and is partnering with space companies like Planet Labs and Starcloud. With first-mover advantage in space AI development and strong fundamentals, Nvidia is expected to generate significant revenue from space applications alongside its terrestrial AI business.

items.title: Prediction: May 21 Will Be a Huge Day for the Stock Market. Here's What You Need to Know.
items.summary: Nvidia's Q1 FY2027 earnings report on May 20 after market close is expected to significantly impact the stock market on May 21. As the AI chip leader, Nvidia's guidance on demand, inference growth, China export progress, and AI's interaction with other technologies will provide crucial visibility into the broader AI sector and tech stocks. The company forecasts 77% revenue growth to $78 billion with 74% gross margins.

items.title: Axe Compute Inc. Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results and Provides Business Update
items.summary: Axe Compute Inc. (NASDAQ: AGPU) announced a $260 million 36-month enterprise contract for dedicated GPU compute capacity, representing approximately $21 million in quarterly revenue once deployed in Q3 2026. The company also appointed Kyle Okamoto as President and Jeremy Yaukey-Witter as CFO. Q1 2026 saw first compute services revenue recognition with a $645k contract liability from customer prepayments, though the company reported a net loss of $7.7 million primarily due to $4.3 million in mark-to-market losses on digital asset holdings.

items.title: SalesCloser Deepens Conversational AI Moat with Dedicated GPU Inference Cluster, Enabling Custom Model Fine-Tuning, Agentic Workflows, and Regulated-Industry Readiness
items.summary: SalesCloser has commissioned a dedicated AI inference cluster using NVIDIA Blackwell-class GPUs hosted on Canadian renewable energy infrastructure. The owned inference layer enables custom model fine-tuning, sophisticated agentic workflows, data sovereignty for regulated industries, reduced vendor dependency, and sustainable operations—positioning the company for enterprise market expansion.

items.title: Nvidia Stock Sank Today. Is It a Buy With Earnings Due Next Week?
items.summary: Nvidia shares dropped 3.3% today, underperforming the broader market decline, after CEO Jensen Huang's China visit yielded no major chip orders. Chinese competitors Tencent and Alibaba are increasing domestic chip production, potentially limiting Nvidia's China expansion. However, with earnings due May 20 and the company's history of beating guidance, today's dip could present a buying opportunity.

items.title: Cerebras CEO Warns US Chip Manufacturing Catch-Up Could Take 15 Years
items.summary: Cerebras Systems went public on Nasdaq at $185 per share, with shares surging 90.28% on strong AI investor enthusiasm. CEO Andrew Feldman stated the company's custom silicon chips can process AI models faster than competitors like Nvidia. However, Feldman warned that US efforts to expand advanced semiconductor manufacturing could take 10-15 years due to supply chain complexity and massive capital requirements. The company relies on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing for chip production.

items.title: Dow Jones Hits 50,000 For the First Time As Nvidia And Broadcom Power Market Indexes Higher
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average crossed 50,000 for the first time ever on May 14, 2026, with all three major indexes gaining approximately 1%. AI chip stocks, particularly Nvidia and Broadcom, drove the market higher following regulatory approval for Nvidia to sell H200 accelerators in China. Cisco jumped 17% on earnings, while Boeing declined 4.5% despite positive trade news.

items.title: Ideal Power Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
items.summary: Ideal Power (NASDAQ:IPWR) reported Q1 2026 results with no commercial revenue, a net loss of $3.6 million, and cash of $16.4 million. The company advanced its B-TRAN® semiconductor technology with multiple new projects including partnerships with an Asia customer for solid-state circuit breakers, a letter of intent with an industry partner for NVIDIA's 800V AI data center architecture, and continued progress with Stellantis on EV applications.

items.title: Stock Market Today: S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq Futures Gain As Trump Clears NVDA's H200 Sales To China— Cisco Systems, Doximity In Focus (UPDATED)
items.summary: U.S. stock futures rose on Thursday as President Trump addresses trade issues with China. Cisco Systems surged 18.8% on better-than-expected Q3 results and raised FY26 guidance. Doximity plunged 21.46% on mixed Q4 results and weak guidance. Grocery Outlet gained 16.41% after beating EPS estimates by 150%. The Fed is expected to hold rates steady in June with 98.6% probability. Nvidia's H200 chip sales to Chinese firms were cleared by the U.S., though Beijing halted deliveries.

items.title: Micron Technology: The AI Platform Moat Wall Street Has Yet to Fully Price
items.summary: Micron Technology has transformed from a cyclical memory supplier into a strategic AI infrastructure platform partner, with stock gains of 700%+ in 12 months. Despite impressive Q2 FY2026 results (revenue $23.86B, non-GAAP EPS $12.20) and strong Q3 guidance, the stock trades at a depressed 7.3x forward P/E compared to semiconductor industry median of 30x. The company's exclusive multi-product qualification for NVIDIA's Vera Rubin platform and high-margin HBM4 production create a competitive moat, though risks include supply normalization in 2027-2028 and geopolitical exposure to China.

items.title: Hagens Berman Files New Securities Class Action Against Super Micro Computer (SMCI) And Its Senior Executives: New Complaint Alleges Additional Corrective Disclosures; Lead Plaintiff Filing Deadline Remains May 26, 2026
items.summary: Hagens Berman filed a new securities class action lawsuit against Super Micro Computer and senior executives, alleging they concealed an illegal scheme to sell billions of dollars worth of advanced AI servers with Nvidia chips to China through a Southeast Asian shell entity, violating U.S. export restrictions. The lawsuit covers investors who purchased stock between February 2, 2024 and March 19, 2026. The fraud was revealed when a DOJ indictment was unsealed on March 19, 2026, charging Super Micro's co-founder with conspiracy to divert servers to China, generating approximately $2.5 billion in sales.

items.title: 'Tip Of The Iceberg': Culper Research Targets Nvidia's Alleged China Problem
items.summary: Culper Research released a short report alleging that Nvidia has a significant undisclosed 'China problem,' claiming over 20% of its FY 2026 compute revenues come from Chinese demand through illegal diversions via Southeast Asian intermediaries, particularly Singapore-based Megaspeed International. The report alleges Megaspeed is secretly financed by Alibaba and connects the operations to a DOJ indictment involving Super Micro Computer, warning Nvidia faces a massive revenue gap as China pivots to domestic alternatives.

items.title: Trump Lands In China With $16 Trillion Of CEO Power — And One Big Ask For Xi
items.summary: President Trump traveled to China for high-stakes talks with President Xi Jinping, bringing a delegation of major U.S. tech CEOs with combined market cap of $16.4 trillion. Trump's primary objective is to request that China 'open up' its markets to American businesses, addressing ongoing disputes over tariffs, trade barriers, AI competition, and market access.

items.title: Navitas Breaks Out on India Deal, Validating High-Power AI Pivot
items.summary: Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) surged 24% on May 11 following a strategic partnership with Cyient to establish gallium nitride (GaN) manufacturing in India. The move validates the company's pivot from low-margin mobile chargers to high-growth AI data centers and EV infrastructure. With strong sequential revenue growth (18% Q4-to-Q1), a robust balance sheet ($221M cash, no debt), and dual GaN/SiC technology capabilities, Navitas is positioned as a key supplier for NVIDIA's next-generation AI platforms. The partnership also provides supply chain diversification and access to India's government-backed semiconductor initiatives.

items.title: Massive News for Marvell Stock Investors: Nvidia Just Changed the Story
items.summary: Marvell Technology is strengthening its position in AI infrastructure through growth in custom silicon, rising data center revenue, and a strategic partnership with Nvidia. However, after a massive stock surge, investors face the question of whether the market has already priced in too much upside.

items.title: Why Sandisk (Not Micron) Could Be the Biggest Winner Of the AI Memory Era
items.summary: Both Sandisk and Micron have benefited from the AI memory boom, with Sandisk up 560% and Micron up 180% year-to-date. However, Sandisk emerges as the more compelling investment due to its multi-year customer contracts, debt-free balance sheet, $6 billion buyback program, and lower capital intensity compared to Micron's aggressive $25+ billion capex spending. While both stocks carry cyclical risk, Sandisk's business model offers better cycle protection.

items.title: Elon Musk Joins Trump's China Trip — Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Not Invited
items.summary: Elon Musk will join President Trump on his China trip, but Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was not invited. The White House is focusing on agriculture and commercial aviation deals, particularly Boeing aircraft orders. Huang's exclusion is notable given Nvidia's central role in U.S.-China tech negotiations and AI chip exports. The snub raises questions about where AI chip exports stand in broader U.S.-China negotiations.

items.title: Cerebras’ $48 Billion IPO Tests the Market’s Inference Bet
items.summary: Cerebras Systems is going public with a $48 billion valuation at $150-$160 per share, up from an initial $115-$125 range, driven by 20x+ institutional oversubscription. The AI chip company's wafer-scale architecture targets inference workloads with major customers OpenAI and AWS validating its technology. However, the valuation at 51x trailing revenue carries risks including customer concentration, TSMC dependency, and Nvidia's competitive response.

items.title: Elon Musk's One Move Turns 'Massive Losses' To 'Significant Profitability': Cathie Wood
items.summary: Cathie Wood highlights how xAI's strategic pivot to monetize its Colossus supercomputer infrastructure through partnerships with Anthropic could transform it from a massive cash burn into a profitable AI cloud utility. The shift from internal AI training expense to external revenue-generating service represents a new template for AI infrastructure economics, potentially turning it into a standalone profit engine rather than just a cost center.

items.title: Palantir Just Showed Why Nvidia Stock Can Plunge on May 21
items.summary: Despite Palantir Technologies crushing earnings expectations with 85% revenue growth and raising guidance, its stock fell 8% due to lofty investor expectations and unsustainable valuation multiples. The article warns that Nvidia faces similar risks ahead of its May 20 earnings report, as history shows game-changing technologies experience bubble-bursting events when adoption expectations prove unrealistic. Additionally, internal competition from major customers developing cheaper alternative chips threatens Nvidia's pricing power.
